system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 2;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 2;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - lower region below wedge
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)  // 3

    ( 0          0         0.005)  // 4
    ( 0          0.09144    0.005)  // 5
    (-0.15242  0.09144    0.005)  // 6
    (-0.15242  0         0.005)   // 7

    // Block 1 - upper region above wedge
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 8
    ( 0.3048     0.09144  -0.005)  // 9
    ( 0.3048     0.1524   -0.005)  // 10
    ( 0          0.1524   -0.005)  // 11

    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.09144   0.005)  // 13
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 14
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type inletOutlet;
        inletValue uniform (10 0 0);
        value uniform (10 0 0);
        faces
        (
            (11 15 14 10)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (8 11 10 9)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error: `log.blockMesh` — Symmetry Plane Not Planar

**Error Message:**
```
Symmetry plane 'bottom' is not planar.
```

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the `bottom` patch contains face `(0 1 5 4)` connecting vertices:
- Vertex 0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- Vertex 1: (0, 0, -0.005)
- Vertex 5: (0, 0.09144, 0.005)
- Vertex 4: (0, 0, 0.005)

As previously identified, this face is non-planar because vertices 0 and 1 have z = -0.005 while vertices 4 and 5 have z = +0.005. The face twists through 3D space.

All previous attempts tried different fixes (splitting into triangles, changing face definitions, merging blocks), but the core geometric issue remains: the current vertex layout makes it impossible to define a planar face for the symmetry plane at y = 0.

### New Approach: Redefine the Geometry Topology

Instead of trying to force the current vertex layout to work, completely restructure the mesh so that the symmetry plane is naturally planar. Here's the recommended approach:

#### Step 1: Redesign the Block Structure

The current design tries to place the symmetry plane at y = 0, but the vertex positions make this impossible with a single quadrilateral face. Instead, **redefine the mesh so that the symmetry plane is at z = 0** (the mid-plane between the top and bottom of the wedge), which allows a clean planar face.

Rearrange the vertices so that the lower block spans z ∈ [-0.005, 0] and the upper block spans z ∈ [0, 0.005]. This way, the symmetry plane at z = 0 becomes a natural planar face.

Update the vertices:

```
// Block 0 - lower region below wedge (z from -0.005 to 0)
// Vertices 0-7 remain as-is except adjust z-coordinate of vertices 4,5,6,7 to z=0
( 0          0         -0.005)  // 0
( 0          0         -0.005)  // 1 — duplicate intentional?
( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 2
(-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)  // 3

( 0          0          0)    // 4 — NEW: z=0
( 0          0.09144      0)    // 5 — NEW: z=0
(-0.15242  0.09144      0)    // 6 — NEW: z=0
(-0.15242  0          0)      // 7 — NEW: z=0

// Block 1 - upper region above wedge (z from 0 to 0.005)
// Vertices 8-15: copy of 0-7 but with z=+0.005
( 0          0          0.005)  // 8 — copy of 0
( 0          0.09144      0.005)  // 9 — copy of 2
( 0.3048     0.09144      0.005)  // 10 — copy of 5
( 0.3048     0.1524      0.005)  // 11 — copy of 6
( 0          0          0.005)  // 12 — copy of 1
( 0.3048     0.09144      0.005)  // 13 — copy of 9
( 0.3048     0.1524      0.005)  // 14 — copy of 10
( 0.3048     0.1524      0.005)  // 15 — copy of 11
```

Then the blocks become:
- Block 0: hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) — lower block at z=-0.005
- Block 1: hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) — upper block at z=+0.005

Now the `bottom` patch face `(0 1 4 7)` lies entirely in the plane z = -0.005 (planar ✓), and the `top` patch face `(2 3 6 5)` lies in the plane z = -0.005 (also planar). The symmetry plane at z = 0 would be represented by the internal face `(4 5 6 7)` between the two blocks, which is planar at z = 0.

#### Step 2: Update Boundary Patch Definitions

With this new topology:
- `bottom`: face `(0 1 4 7)` — external bottom face of lower block (z = -0.005)
- `top`: face `(2 3 6 5)` — external top face of lower block (z = -0.005)
- `wedge`: face `(1 2 6 5)` — the slanted wedge surface
- `outlet`: face `(9 10 14 13)` — right-facing outlet
- `inlet`: face `(0 4 7 3)` — left-facing inlet
- `frontAndBack`: faces `(0 3 2 1)` and `(8 11 10 9)` — the two side walls

The key change is moving the symmetry plane from y = 0 to z = 0, which allows a clean planar face definition.

### Error 2: Cascading from Failed `blockMesh`

This error is purely a consequence of the failed mesh generation. Once `blockMesh` succeeds with the redesigned topology above, `rhoCentralFoam` will be able to find the mesh files and run.

---

## Summary of Required Changes

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Non-planar symmetry face `(0 1 5 4)` | **Completely redesign the vertex layout** so that the symmetry plane is at z = 0. Adjust vertex 4,5,6,7 to have z = 0 (instead of ±0.005), and set vertices 8,9,10,11,12,13,14,15 as copies of 0-7 with z = +0.005. Then define `bottom` as `(0 1 4 7)` (z = -0.005), `top` as `(2 3 6 5)` (z = -0.005), and the internal face `(4 5 6 7)` will serve as the symmetry plane at z = 0. Update all patch face lists accordingly. |

This structural change ensures the symmetry plane face is inherently planar, resolving the root cause of the error. All other files (controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, boundary conditions, physical properties) appear correct and ready to run once the mesh is successfully generated.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Redesign entire vertex layout so symmetry plane is planar at z=0; Move vertices 4,5,6,7 to z=0 coordinates instead of z=±0.005; Set vertices 0,1,2,3 to z=-0.005; Copy vertices 0-7 to vertices 8-15 with z=+0.005; Define block 0 as hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) with z-range -0.005 to 0; Define block 1 as hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) with z-range 0 to 0.005; Change bottom patch face from (0 1 5 4) to (0 1 4 7) which is planar at z=-0.005; Change top patch face from (11 15 14 10) to (2 3 6 5) which is also planar at z=-0.005; Keep inlet as face (0 4 7 3); Keep outlet as face (9 10 14 13); Keep wedge as face (1 2 6 5); Keep frontAndBack as faces (0 3 2 1) and (8 11 10 9)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 2 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
